summary: High‑energy 1977 Hartford show captured on a solid audience tape; crisp later sections and a standout 20‑minute Sugaree make it a must‑listen despite a muddy start.
review: The performance is described as smoky, energetic and well‑played, with highlights like a marathon Sugaree and an inspired Samson & Delilah jam. The audience recording is generally praised for its crispness after the opening hiccups caused by mic‑stand problems, making it one of the better mid‑’70s AUD tapes. Overall fans recommend the show even though the first few minutes are a bit muddy.

ai_rating.rationale: Reviews uniformly praise the musical performance (standout songs, energy) and rate the recording as good to excellent despite early muddiness, yielding a high combined score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Early portion muddy due to mic‑stand issue; later sections crisp and clear, overall an above‑average mid‑70s audience tape.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Exciting first set that builds into a superb second set run; fans note an amazing 20‑minute Sugaree and sustained high energy throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Mic‑stand trouble at the start caused missed opening songs, but his tone improves later and is praised.
